Many of the properties are specific to the ECOMP continuous integration environment.  Remove all properties that are not used by ONAP.
Profiles are used for ECOMP testing and should be removed
Remove any plugin that is not directly required by your code, which is probably only swagger generation.  The oparent pom will handle everything else.

Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString() will return String directly

It's very rare to see String() constructor used, and it's not needed here.
Unnecessary String() contructor if using encodeToString()
Remove these "hello world" messages.  Ideally, remove EELF messages and use pure SLF4J.

Tabs are not allowed by the ONAP coding guidelines.

https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Java+code+style
Use a REST client and not raw HTTP.  There's one already configured in JerseyConfiguration.
These tests should also verify the HTTP status code (400, 401, etc.)

As Brad mentioned, correct parameter order is assertEquals(expected, actual).  If there is a mismatch, the exception will be misleading;  "Expected <badValue> but got <sdnchost>" when you really want "Expected <sdnchost> but got <badValue>".
